title: RPYR-0007 — `seq * n` aborts (SIGABRT) on an unallocatable-but-in-range size: the shared `SequenceExt::mul` guard has a hole (`sequence.rs`)
toolkit_class: allocator-abort (guard converts >isize::MAX to MemoryError but not merely-unallocatable sizes)
guarded_twin: n/a — this IS the shared guard; the fix is to use try_reserve/checked allocation and map failure to MemoryError, which fixes tuple/list/bytes/bytearray at once
notes: SEVERITY NOTE: this is an allocator-policy abort more than a logic bug, and is broad/shared. SequenceExt::mul's guard (sequence.rs:103) rejects totals exceeding isize::MAX BYTES (~9.2 EB) -> MemoryError, but a merely-unallocatable request such as (1,)*(10**12) = 8 TB is < isize::MAX, so it passes the guard and Vec::with_capacity(n*len) hits handle_alloc_error -> SIGABRT (exit 134). CPython raises MemoryError. The clean fix is Vec::try_reserve_exact + map_err(|_| vm.new_memory_error(...)). Boundary confirmed: (1,)*sys.maxsize DOES raise MemoryError (the guard catches >isize::MAX bytes); (1,)*(10**12) aborts (under the byte ceiling).
